commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e981655e98e5e8cd40d122f2c0068b34d109a273)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `chat.send` fallback that returns absolute managed inbound PDF paths when sandbox staging throws or is incomplete, plus regression tests for fallback and mixed-batch rejection.

PR surface: Source +44, Tests +161. Total +205 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main saves non-image PDF offloads, then `prestageMediaPathOffloads` can fail before dispatch when sandbox staging is incomplete or size-gated; I did not run a live gateway repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is a narrow repair in the gateway staging helper plus focused regression proof.

target.title: fix(gateway): pass managed inbound PDFs through when sandbox staging fails (#90097)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90097. `chat.send` offloads large attachments to the managed inbound media store, then — for a session with a sandbox workspace — force-copies every offloaded non-image ref into the sandbox via `stageSandboxMedia`. If that **optional** staging was unavailable or incomplete, `prestageMediaPathOffloads` **deleted the media buffers and failed the whole send with a 5xx** (`UNAVAILABLE`) — even for an already-managed inbound PDF that is safe to read host-side. A Control-UI-uploaded PDF could fail to send (`impact:message-loss`).

The no-sandbox branch already passes absolute managed paths through. This change extends that safe shape to the sandbox **failure** path: when staging throws or is incomplete, fall back to the absolute managed paths **iff every** non-image offloaded ref is a managed-inbound `application/pdf`. Reuses the existing `resolveInboundMediaReference` allow-check (the same predicate `stageSandboxMedia` uses) + the PDF mime type. With `MediaWorkspaceDir` unset, the managed media dir is a default media-understanding local root, so the absolute path resolves host-side — the sandboxed agent never opens the PDF itself.

Safety: gated **all-or-nothing** — a single non-managed or non-PDF ref keeps the previous delete + 5xx behavior, so no arbitrary local path or unresolved `media://` ref can pass through. Success path and the oversized-attachment 4xx are unchanged; managed buffers are **not** deleted on the fallback. Scope is `application/pdf` only (per the issue), and oversized managed PDFs keep their existing 4xx.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: an already-managed inbound PDF send fails with a 5xx (and its media buffer is deleted) when optional sandbox staging is unavailable/incomplete.

Real environment tested: ran the real `resolveInboundMediaReference` trust predicate (the gate the fallback relies on) against real on-disk files under a real managed media store (`$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/media/inbound`), on macOS, Node 22.22.3, off latest `origin/main`, then evaluated the same all-or-nothing managed-PDF condition the patched `prestageMediaPathOffloads` applies.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: wrote a real PDF under the managed inbound store + an arbitrary PDF outside it; called the real `resolveInboundMediaReference` on the managed path, an outside path, and a `../../` path-escape; then ran the fallback decision on an all-managed-PDF batch, a mixed PDF+zip batch, and an arbitrary-path batch.

Evidence after fix (copied runtime output):

```text
[setup] managed media-store inbound dir = /tmp/.../media/inbound
[predicate] real managed inbound PDF      : MANAGED (non-null)
[predicate] arbitrary local PDF (outside)  : rejected (null)
[predicate] path-escape ../../outside.pdf  : rejected (null)
[fallback] all-managed-PDF batch        -> PASS THROUGH managed path (no delete, send proceeds)
[fallback] mixed batch (PDF + zip)      -> NO fallback (delete + 5xx, all-or-nothing holds)
[fallback] arbitrary-path PDF batch     -> NO fallback (untrusted path rejected)
[result] OK — real managed PDF is recognized and would pass through on staging failure
```

Observed result after fix: a real managed inbound PDF is recognized by the trust predicate and passes through on staging failure; an arbitrary path and a path-escape are rejected; a mixed (non-PDF) batch does not pass through. So the managed-PDF send proceeds with the absolute managed path instead of deleting the buffer and returning 5xx, while the trust boundary holds.

What was not tested: a full live gateway `chat.send` smoke against a real sandbox with induced staging failure (covered by the reporter's own evidence in the issue and by the deterministic regression tests below, which drive the real `chat.send` → `prestageMediaPathOffloads` control flow).

## Supplemental checks (not a substitute for the proof above)

- `pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts` — 212 pass, incl. 3 new tests: fallback on incomplete staging, fallback on a staging throw, and a mixed-batch negative that still deletes + 5xx. The two fallback tests fail without this patch.
- `src/auto-reply/reply.triggers...stages-inbound-media...test.ts` — 3 pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` + `pnpm tsgo:test:src` exit 0; `oxfmt --check` + oxlint clean.

## Files
- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` — managed-inbound-PDF fallback in `prestageMediaPathOffloads` (+ 1 import).
- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts` — 3 regression tests.
target.author: harjothkhara
